methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $1.0B - $23.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 20.6x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Cerebras
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Public), post-money $23B

private.name: Credo Semiconductor
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Public), post-money $5B+

private.name: Aeva Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Public), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
Ouster is a publicly traded semiconductor company with current valuation of $1.5B and $500M+ in total capital raised. The peer set consists of 3 public and 8 private companies in the Chips & Semiconductors sector, including public comparables Cerebras ($23.0B), Credo Semiconductor ($5.0B), and Aeva Technologies ($1.0B).

Peer-median valuation was the applicable triangulation method. The median across the 3 same-sector, same-stage public peers yields $5.0B, with a range spanning $1.0B to $23.0B. Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales multiples was not feasible; while the broader Semiconductors sector trades at a 20.6x median P/S across ALAB, NVDA, and AMD, Ouster's revenue is not on file, precluding multiple application.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $4.2B (low) to $5.8B (high), centered at $5.0B median. This implies material upside from the current $1.5B mark. Key uncertainties include the wide dispersion among existing public peers—a 23x spread between the minimum and maximum—and the absence of revenue disclosure, which prevents triangulation via conventional multiples. Stage-specific comparables and performance metrics relative to the peer set would meaningfully constrain this range.
